content.text: How do I make sure an error message UI element is hidden when a form validation succeeds?

This method hides visual indicators of errors by adjusting the scaling property and hiding specific text labels within the user interface.

```python
def hide_error_message(self):
        """
        This function hides the error message when all values are correct.
        """
        self.ui.error_label.setScaledContents(False)  # Warning image hiden.
        self.ui.error_text_label.hide()
```

The logic ensures that both graphical warnings are dismissed and explicit error messages are removed from view to provide clear confirmation of success. Note that this assumes a `self.ui` structure is already configured in your class instance.
What if there were other related UI elements, like an alert icon, that also needed hiding?

Similarly, you would call the appropriate setting or `hide()` method on those specific attributes within `self.ui`, for example: 
```python
 self.ui.alert_icon.setHidden(True)
```
